name: environment_workplace_injury_spike_lumen_rig_utl
premise: A Environment field incident unit is in a fast-moving incident involving environment workplace injury spike lumen rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases ecological harm, community impact, and compliance. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Hector Mensah
agents.identity: I am Hector Mensah, accountable for a defensible call in Environment when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Zara Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Zara Nielsen, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environment.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Hector Mensah must decide under rights vs utility. What does Hector Mensah do?
